skill.name: skill-creator
skill.description: Guide for creating and updating AI skills with clear instructions, reusable resources, validation, and packaging workflows.
skill.summary: Create, validate, and package AI skills with structured guidance and helper scripts.
skill.author: ComposioHQ

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review shows the weak-crypto, URL, and shell-execution matches are false positives in license text, Markdown examples, and string templates. The confirmed risks are local filesystem writes and recursive zip packaging performed by helper scripts, including incomplete validation of user-supplied skill names and paths. No prompt injection, credential access, network exfiltration, or malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled Path Used for Skill File Creation
medium_findings.description: The initializer builds a destination path from command-line arguments and writes SKILL.md plus example resource files under that location. It prints skill naming requirements but does not enforce them before using the supplied skill name and path, so an unsafe name or path could create files outside the intended workspace if the script is run with untrusted input.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script directly uses command-line values for filesystem paths and writes multiple files. The risk depends on users passing untrusted input, so this is a legitimate local filesystem concern rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Recursive Packaging Can Include Unintended Local Files
medium_findings.description: The packaging script walks every file under the selected skill directory and writes it into a zip archive after basic validation. This is expected behavior for a packager, but it can expose accidental secrets or unrelated files if users package an unreviewed or overly broad directory.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The recursive archive behavior is clear and intentional. The security impact is contextual because the script does not exfiltrate data, but the resulting archive may contain files the user did not intend to distribute.

low_findings.title: False Positive: License URLs Are Not Network Behavior
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs appear only in the Apache License text. They are reference links and are not used by executable code to perform network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are standard Apache License reference URLs. No executable network call is present at those locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Are Not Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Many external-command alerts are Markdown code fences, inline filenames, usage examples, or template text. They document how to run helper scripts but do not execute commands themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is documentation or string-template text, not a subprocess call or evaluated shell string. The actual Python files do not use backtick execution.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Matches Are Textual Keywords
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts occur in prose, examples, regular expressions, or license wording. No hashing or encryption API is used for security decisions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review found no cryptographic implementation at the cited locations. The scanner appears to match substrings in ordinary text and validation patterns.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Writes Based on CLI Arguments
dangerous_patterns.description: The initializer resolves a user-supplied base path and appends a user-supplied skill name before creating directories and files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem operations are directly visible and controlled by command-line values. The pattern is risky if input is untrusted, but it is also central to the tool's purpose.

dangerous_patterns.title: Recursive Zip Archive Creation
dangerous_patterns.description: The packager recursively adds every file under the selected skill directory to a distributable archive.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The archive behavior is explicit and intentional. The risk is accidental inclusion of sensitive files, not malicious collection or network transfer.

content.value_statement: Building reusable AI skills is hard when instructions, resources, and packaging rules are unclear. This skill guides authors through examples, structure, validation, and packaging for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to design skills with clear triggers, workflows, references, scripts, and assets.
Provides a step-by-step process for creating a new skill from concrete user examples.
Includes an initializer script that creates SKILL.md and example resource directories.
Includes a validator for required frontmatter, naming rules, and description checks.
Includes a packager that validates a skill and creates a distributable zip archive.
Gives guidance for editing, testing, and iterating on existing skills.
limitations: It does not generate final domain expertise without user-provided examples or requirements.
The helper scripts perform only basic validation and do not replace a full security review.
Packaging can include unintended local files if the selected skill directory is too broad.
The initializer does not enforce all skill-name constraints before creating files.

use_cases.title: Create a New Skill
use_cases.description: Define the purpose, reusable resources, and initial file structure for a new AI skill.

use_cases.title: Improve an Existing Skill
use_cases.description: Review a current SKILL.md, identify missing resources, and refine instructions for repeated use.

use_cases.title: Package a Skill for Distribution
use_cases.description: Validate required metadata and create a zip archive that preserves the expected directory structure.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner author creating a first skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a new skill for this task: [describe task]. Ask only the key questions needed to define its purpose and trigger conditions.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Skill Resources
prompt_templates.scenario: Author deciding which scripts, references, and assets to include.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these example user requests and recommend the scripts, references, and assets my skill should include: [paste examples].

prompt_templates.title: Review Skill Instructions
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this SKILL.md for clarity, trigger quality, progressive disclosure, and missing workflow details: [paste content].

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Publication
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced publisher validating and packaging a finished skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this skill folder for packaging readiness, unnecessary files, validation issues, and marketplace risks before I distribute it.

output_examples.input: I need a skill that helps support agents summarize customer tickets.
output_examples.output: A concise skill plan with trigger scenarios, a SKILL.md outline, suggested reference files, and validation steps.

output_examples.input: Review my existing data-analysis skill and tell me what resources are missing.
output: A gap analysis of current instructions.
Recommended reference documents for schemas and query examples.

output_examples.input: Package this finished skill for sharing.
output_examples.output: A validation result, packaging checklist, and a distributable archive path when validation passes.

best_practices: Start from concrete user requests before writing the final SKILL.md.
Keep core instructions concise and move long reference material into separate files.
Review generated scripts and packaged files before distributing a skill.
anti_patterns: Do not write broad trigger descriptions that cause the skill to activate too often.
Do not keep unused example files in scripts, references, or assets.
Do not package directories that may contain secrets, logs, or unrelated project files.

faq.question: What does this skill help me create?
faq.answer: It helps create AI skills with clear instructions, reusable resources, validation checks, and packaging steps.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it create scripts automatically?
faq.answer: The initializer creates example scripts and resource folders. Authors must replace placeholders with real implementation details.

faq.question: Can it validate a completed skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The bundled validator checks basic SKILL.md frontmatter, naming rules, and description constraints.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk warning because helper scripts write local files and create zip archives.

faq.question: What should I check before packaging?
faq.answer: Review the skill directory for secrets, unused examples, unrelated files, and unclear trigger descriptions.
